channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Thai police arrest driver and work to identify victims of the school bus fire that killed 23
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai police have arrested the driver of a bus carrying young students and teachers that caught fire and killed 23 in suburban Bangkok, as families arrived in the capital Wednesday to help identify their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After pressing an Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire, the Biden administration shifts its message
item.description: The Biden administration says there's a significant difference between Israeli actions that have expanded its war against the Iranian-backed militant groups Hamas and Hezbollah and Iran’s retaliatory missile attack against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank risk crossing Israel's separation barrier to flee failing economy
item.description: A year of war in the Gaza Strip has reverberated across the West Bank, where the World Bank warns the economy is at risk of collapse because of Israeli restrictions barring Palestinian laborers from entering the country to work and the biggest wave of violence in decades.
item.dc:creator: Jack Jeffery, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: France's prime minister vows to curb debt as he faced baptism of fire at heckled parliament
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Lawmakers gave new French Prime Minister Michel Barnier a rough ride, heckling him constantly, as he laid out his proposed policies in an inaugural speech to parliament on Tuesday, a baptism of fire that highlighted how difficult governing will be for his minority government.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's new Prime Minister Ishiba vows to push a strong defense under the Japan-US alliance
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan's new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba pledged to stick to the vital Japan-U.S. alliance while calling for it to be more equitable after he took office Tuesday vowing to tackle a slow economy and regain public trust before an upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A plane carrying Brazil President Lula lands safely at Mexico City airport after circling for hours
item.description: ZUMPANGO, Mexico (AP) — An airplane carrying Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva back to Brasilia landed safely on Tuesday at Mexico City’s Felipe Angeles airport after circling for hours due to a technical problem.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and MARTIN SILVA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles at Israel on Tuesday evening, causing scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel, but Israeli authorities said there were no injuries. An Israeli security official said most of the missiles were intercepted, though some managed to land.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Iran attacks ratchet up Middle East conflict: 5 takeaways
item.description: The Middle East is a powder keg after Iran launched nearly 200 missiles toward Israel Wednesday, with the United States vowing "severe consequences" for the action. No deaths in Israel were immediately assessed, and U.S. officials said the missiles — aimed at Israel and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says Iran 'will pay' for missile attack
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iran “will pay” for the 180 missiles launched toward his country on Tuesday. “This evening, Iran made a big mistake — and it will pay for it,” the leader said during a security Cabinet meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, The Hill

item.title: Trump warns of 'global catastrophe' following Iranian missile attack on Israel
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday bashed President Biden and Vice President Harris in the wake of an Iranian missile attack on Israel, arguing if Harris is elected in November, it would lead to mass chaos on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: White House calls Iranian missile attack 'significant escalation'
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan on Tuesday called Iran's missile attack against Israel a "significant escalation" and said the Biden administration is determining how to respond.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as North America's first female head of state
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in at a joint session of the country’s Congress on Tuesday, becoming North America’s first female head of state.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Israel under attack: 2 gunmen kill 6 civilians, injure others near Tel Aviv
item.description: A mass shooting by at least two individuals near Tel Aviv has killed and injured multiple people — an incident authorities believe is terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels launch drone boat that hits ship in Red Sea as missile strikes another
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels launched an explosive-loaded drone that crashed into one ship Tuesday in the Red Sea and a missile that exploded against another.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian fighter jet flies within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The U.S. military is slamming Russia for an “unsafe” encounter in the skies off the coast of Alaska after footage released Monday shows a Kremlin fighter jet cutting off an Air Force F-16. The incident, which took place Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Mysteries of universe revealed? Hardly. But CERN still fascinates, discovers on its 70th anniversary
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The research center that is home to the world’s largest particle accelerator is celebrating its 70th anniversary on Tuesday, with the physicists who run it aiming to unlock secrets about dark matter and other mysteries to promote science for peace in today's conflict-darkened world.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian national indicted in ransomware attacks in US
item.description: A Russian national is accused in a series of ransomware attacks in Texas and other parts of the U.S. over a seven-year period, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Iran preparing missile attack on Israel, White House says
item.description: Iran is preparing to fire ballistic missiles at Israel, according to a senior White House official, who said the attack could be launched "imminently." "We are actively supporting defensive preparations to defend Israel against this attack," the official said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels are looking to gain from continuing conflict in the Middle East
item.description: Since Israel intensified its campaign against Hezbollah in Lebanon, Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels have been quick to show they're an important player in the complex conflicts convulsing the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Thailand school bus bursts into flames outside Bangkok, 23 feared dead, officials say
item.description: A school bus burst into flames on Tuesday while carrying more than 40 students and teachers on a field trip near Bangkok, Thailand, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A cruise ship bound on a round-the-world voyage leaves Belfast after a 4-month delay
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cruise ship taking passengers on a 3 ½-year around-the-world voyage has finally sailed from Belfast's dockside, after spending four months stuck in the Northern Ireland capital.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tibetan group in India protests against China and human rights situation in homeland
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Dozens of Tibetans living in India protested outside China's embassy on Tuesday against the human rights situation in their homeland, which China annexed in 1951.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel begins limited ground incursions in Lebanon
item.description: Israel has begun limited ground incursions into Lebanon for the first time, a dramatic escalation in the 11-month conflict with the Iranian-backed militia group Hezbollah and the start of a larger and potentially long fight against the group.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russian fighter jet intercepted near Alaska by NORAD forces after ‘unsafe, unprofessional’ interaction
item.description: North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) released a video showing the dangerous moment a Russian Su-35 fighter pilot flew right in front of a NORAD F-16 fighter pilot over the Alaska Air Defense Zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding, landslides kill nearly 200 in Nepal
item.description: At least 193 people have been confirmed dead following severe flooding and landslides in Nepal, where heavy weekend rainfall was reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders weigh in on 'legitimate' issue of illegal immigration: 'Must be stopped'
item.description: The European Union allows for free movement between member states, making any immigration policy on national borders difficult to enforce while the continental border remains porous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in hiding with extra security following Hezbollah leader's death: report
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was placed in a secure location after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah's killing in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding and landslides in Nepal kill at least 66 people, with as many again still missing
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Flooding and landslides caused by continuous rainfall has killed at least 66 people in Nepal, and a further 69 are missing, officials said Saturday. Rain has been pouring down since Friday night and is expected to continue over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reward offered for Iranian in alleged Bolton assassination plot
item.description: Iranian Vice-President Mohammad Javad Zarif rejected accusations that Iran has tried to assassinate government critics overseas.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu vows to use 'full force' against Hezbollah and dims hopes for a cease-fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday vowed to carry out “full force” strikes against Hezbollah until it ceases firing rockets across the border, dimming hopes for a cease-fire proposal put forth by U.S. and European officials.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
